title: Secret Bytes, Constant-Time Decisions, and Memory Lifecycle · Runtime Contract: Crypto Go Secret String Copy
summary: A Go service converts secrets to immutable strings, logs parse failures with raw material, compares tags with ordinary equality, and promises memory erasure the runtime cannot prove. Predict the asset, adversary, security property, trust boundary, and falsifying evidence before opening the governing standard. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound secret copies, constant-time comparison scope, redaction, and honest erasure claims; cap bytes, work, memory, time, attempts, concurrency, and retained secret material before use; then remove one transfer gate and state exactly which production claim is no longer supported.
